findings.description: Page 1 has a two-panel layout (left panel with audio troubleshooting, right panel with welcome message) that is collapsed into a single column in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows a side-by-side layout with audio troubleshooting on the left and 'Welcome to the CHPP Steering Committee Meeting' on the right. HTML renders these sequentially as a single column.

findings.description: The decorative cork-board background texture behind the welcome text is missing in the HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows a textured cork-board background on the right panel. HTML shows plain white background.

findings.description: The column of wildlife/habitat images on the right side of the Coastal Habitat Protection Plan timeline slide are repositioned to appear above the table in the HTML instead of alongside it
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows images in a vertical strip on the right side next to the timeline table. HTML renders these images stacked vertically above the table content.

findings.description: The Coastal Habitat Protection Plan timeline page has a side-by-side layout (table on left, images on right) that is collapsed to single column in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows the timeline table occupying about 80% width with habitat photos in a column on the right. HTML shows images stacked above the table in a single column.

findings.description: Duplicate <main> tags create a nested structure, though this is a structural HTML issue rather than a content issue.
findings.evidence: The HTML has <main> nested inside another <main> element.

findings.description: The agenda content on page 2 appears both as plain text in the source metadata/title area and as a structured table in the HTML. The agenda items from the PDF's page 2 are rendered once in the HTML table, but the raw text block at the top of the document description (before page 1 content) repeats agenda-like text that duplicates the title slide content.
findings.evidence: The PDF page 1 title text 'Welcome to the CHPP Steering Committee Meeting' and 'Say "Hi" in the chat box...' appear in both the document-level description and in the page 1 section of the HTML, but this is due to the source document structure itself providing both a text extraction and visual content.

findings.description: Alt text for side images on page 3 is partially fabricated/speculative. The PDF shows a strip of nature photos along the right side, but the alt text descriptions are somewhat speculative about specific content.
findings.evidence: Alt text says 'Egret wading in wetland', 'Collage of coastal habitats including oyster reef, seagrass, and sandy bottom', 'Fish swimming over reef' - these are reasonable guesses for coastal habitat images but cannot be fully verified from the PDF rendering.

findings.description: The document has the welcome slide title as h1 ('Welcome to the CHPP Steering Committee Meeting') while 'AUDIO TROUBLESHOOTING' is rendered as h2. In the PDF, both are prominent headings on the same page, but this hierarchy choice is reasonable.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 has 'AUDIO TROUBLESHOOTING' as a bold header and the welcome title as the main decorative heading.

findings.description: The agenda table in HTML adds column headers 'Time' and 'Agenda Item' that are not explicitly present in the PDF. The PDF presents the agenda as a formatted list with times and descriptions but without formal column headers.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 shows 'Agenda' as the heading with time-description pairs listed below. The HTML adds <th>Time</th> and <th>Agenda Item</th> headers. Similarly, the timeline table headers 'Action' and 'Completion Date' do exist in the PDF.
